..summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.ownsExecutor is always false — the lifecycle abstraction it guards can never activate

evidence: Only one constructor exists (line 84): `this.ownsExecutor = false;` — no code path ever sets it to true
close() (line 330) contains `if (ownsExecutor && executor instanceof AutoCloseable ac)` — this branch is unreachable dead code
The field and the conditional suggest an ownership-transfer constructor was planned but never implemented
detail.suggestion: Remove the ownsExecutor field and the dead branch in close(). If executor lifecycle ownership is needed in future, re-introduce it as a second constructor parameter or factory method at that time rather than carrying a permanently-false flag.

..summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles and exploreMultiSeed are pure one-liner delegations to CollocateProfileComparator with no added value

evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles body: `return comparator.compareCollocateProfiles(seedNouns, minLogDice, maxPerNoun);` — identical signature, zero logic added
SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed body: `return comparator.exploreMultiSeed(seeds, relationConfig, minLogDice, topCollocates, minShared);` — identical signature, zero logic added
ExplorationHandlers holds a SemanticFieldExplorer and calls both methods; it could depend on CollocateProfileComparator directly for these two operations, removing the empty intermediary hop
detail.suggestion: Either let ExplorationHandlers depend directly on CollocateProfileComparator for compareCollocateProfiles/exploreMultiSeed (eliminating the pass-through), or merge CollocateProfileComparator back into SemanticFieldExplorer as private methods since the public surface adds no value. If SemanticFieldExplorer must remain the single dependency of ExplorationHandlers, add a brief doc comment explaining why the delegation is intentional (e.g. lifecycle or future policy hook).

..summary: WordSketchApiServer stores executor and indexPath as instance fields but neither is read after constructor delegation

evidence: Fields declared at lines 35-37: `private final QueryExecutor executor` and `private final String indexPath`
Both are assigned in the constructor (lines 45-46) and then only passed into SketchHandlers/SemanticFieldExplorer — never referenced again in any method
A developer scanning the class would assume these fields are used for something, then spend time searching for their usage
detail.suggestion: Remove both fields. Use local variables in the constructor when passing executor and indexPath to SketchHandlers and SemanticFieldExplorer, since no post-construction access occurs.

..summary: concordance/examples 'raw' field falls back to sentence text on null, while bcql endpoint defaults to empty string

evidence: handleConcordanceExamples: exMap.put("raw", r.rawXml() != null ? r.rawXml() : r.sentence()) — raw becomes the sentence string when rawXml is null
handleBcqlQueryPost: resultMap.put("raw", r.rawXml() != null ? r.rawXml() : "") — raw becomes empty string when rawXml is null
Both endpoints return CollocateResult objects and expose a 'raw' field, so consumers expect the same null contract
detail.suggestion: Align handleConcordanceExamples to use the same fallback as handleBcqlQueryPost: r.rawXml() != null ? r.rawXml() : "". If the sentence text is also needed, expose it via a separate 'sentence' field (which is already present).

..summary: ExploreOptions.headPos and collocatePos are set by handler but never read by SemanticFieldExplorer

evidence: ExploreOptions record (lines 13-14) declares headPos and collocatePos fields
ExplorationHandlers lines 88-92 reads headPosition/collocatePosition from RelationConfig and passes them into ExploreOptions
SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern unpacks only opts.topCollocates(), opts.nounsPerCollocate(), opts.minLogDice(), opts.minShared() — never touches opts.headPos() or opts.collocatePos()
executeSurfacePattern in QueryExecutor no longer accepts these position hints after the interface cleanup, confirming the fields are now orphaned
detail.suggestion: Remove headPos and collocatePos from ExploreOptions. If position hints are ever needed again, pass them through RelationConfig directly or derive them from the pattern inside the executor.

..summary: /api/semantic-field accepts 'seeds' parameter but puts 'nouns' key in the response body

evidence: handleSemanticField line ~194: String nounsParam = HttpApiUtils.requireParam(exchange, params, "seeds") — input key is 'seeds'
handleSemanticField line 230: response.put("nouns", new ArrayList<>(result.getNouns())) — response key is 'nouns'
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti line 174 uses response.put("seeds", ...) consistently — making the /api/semantic-field endpoint the only outlier
detail.suggestion: Change line 230 from response.put("nouns", ...) to response.put("seeds", ...) to match the input parameter name and align with the multi-seed endpoint convention.

..summary: Full-sketch and single-relation sketch endpoints return structurally incompatible response shapes

evidence: handleFullSketchImpl returns {lemma, relations: {id: {name, cql, collocations:[...]}}} — collocations nested two levels deep under a relation-keyed map
handleRelationQueryInternal returns {lemma, relation, collocations:[...]} — collocations flat at the top level, no per-relation metadata
Both are served under /api/sketch/{lemma} and /api/sketch/{lemma}/{relation} — a client switching between the two must handle two incompatible schemas
detail.suggestion: Have the single-relation endpoint wrap its collocations inside the same per-relation object shape as the full endpoint: {lemma, relations: {<id>: {name, cql, collocations:[...]}}}. This makes the single-relation response a predictable subset of the full-sketch response.

..summary: Three coexisting field-access strategies in the model package: public fields, private+getters, and records

evidence: AdjectiveProfile declares `public final String adjective`, `public final int presentInCount`, `public final double avgLogDice`, etc. — all accessed directly as dn.adjective, adj.presentInCount across the codebase
CoreCollocate, Edge, DiscoveredNoun follow the same public-field pattern
ExplorationResult declares all fields private with explicit getters: getSeed(), getSeedCollocates(), getDiscoveredNouns(), getCoreCollocates()
ComparisonResult similarly uses private fields + getters: getNouns(), getAllAdjectives()
QueryResults.SnippetResult, KwicResult, CollocateResult are Java records; ExploreOptions is a Java record — accessor syntax differs again
Callers switch between styles in the same method body, e.g. ExplorationHandlers line 307 uses dn.noun (public field) and line 107 uses result.getSeed() (getter)
detail.suggestion: Converge all model value types to Java records. Convert AdjectiveProfile, CoreCollocate, Edge, and DiscoveredNoun to records; their constructors map 1:1 to record components. This aligns them with ExploreOptions and the QueryResults subtypes, eliminates the public mutable surface (the fields are final but still public), and unifies access syntax. ExplorationResult and ComparisonResult have richer behaviour (filtering/edge-building methods) and could remain as classes with private fields + getters, or become records with additional methods — document the rationale either way.

..summary: Dead null checks for grammarConfig inside handler methods that can never be reached after constructor hardening

evidence: Constructor (line ~33) throws IllegalArgumentException for null grammarConfig, making the field non-null for all reachable instances
handleSemanticFieldExplore (line 56) and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (line 142) both still contain 'if (grammarConfig == null) { sendError(500, ...) }' branches that are unreachable dead code
detail.suggestion: Remove both dead null guards from the handler methods; the constructor precondition is the correct enforcement point.

..summary: handleSemanticFieldExplore assembles domain objects (bcqlPattern, reverseCollocatePattern, ExploreOptions) inside the HTTP handler instead of delegating to a service/factory

evidence: Lines 87-100: bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed); reverseCollocatePattern = ...; headPos = ...; ExploreOptions opts = new ExploreOptions(...) all assembled in-handler
An explicit TODO at line 84 acknowledges this: 'domain object assembly should be delegated to a factory/service rather than assembled in the HTTP handler'
detail.suggestion: Introduce a method or factory on SemanticFieldExplorer (or a new ExploreRequest builder) that accepts a RelationConfig and seed string and returns the assembled ExploreOptions + patterns, so the handler only parses HTTP params and delegates.
